text.title: Per-tracker Approval Gate Mechanics
text.summary: The agent blocks on a tracker-specific approval signal — Linear emoji reaction, Jira slash command, or GitHub label — and resumes only when the signal is detected from a non-bot user.

section.title: Detecting the approval signal
section.p: Each tracker exposes a different idiom for human acknowledgement. The agent's polling/webhook handler must (a) listen on the right event, (b) reject signals from agent identities, (c) idempotently record the gate clearance against the ticket so a second emoji or repeat label does not retrigger the workflow.

rule.statement: The agent MUST treat the gate as cleared only when an approval event originates from an identity NOT matching `^(claude|copilot|devin|cursor|rovo|duo|bot)-.*$` and is one of: Linear `:thumbsup:` reaction on the agent's spec comment, Jira issue comment matching `^/agent\s+approve\b`, or GitHub issue labelled `agent:approved` by a user with write access. Self-approval, agent-to-agent approval, and approval before the spec comment is posted MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Atlassian's "agents respect your permissions, project configurations, workflows, and audit trails" and GitHub's branch-protection-style separation of approver from author both encode the same invariant: an automated actor cannot satisfy a gate that exists to bound automation.

example.code: # templates/approval-gate.yaml (excerpt) approval: linear: event: reaction.added on_comment: agent_spec_comment_id emoji: thumbsup actor_filter: human_only jira: event: comment.created body_match: '^/agent\s+approve\b' actor_filter: project_role_developer github: event: issue.labeled label: agent:approved actor_filter: write_permission
example.note: Each tracker maps to one event with an actor filter; the agent's `proceed` only fires on the union.
A second agent (or the same agent on a stale comment) trips the gate; nothing prevents self-approval.

antipattern.description: Agent treats absence of dissent (no comments for N minutes) as implicit approval.
antipattern.reason: "Silence = consent" reliably ships wrong-spec PRs when the reporter is asleep, on PTO, or in a different timezone; the gate must be an explicit positive signal.

section.reference: Per-tracker approval mapping consumed by the agent runtime.
Linear: agents as first-class assignees with comment-based handoff.
Atlassian: agents inherit Jira permissions, workflows, audit trails.
GitHub community: enforcing the link/label contract on agent PRs.
